methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Summit Nanotech is a Series A-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Amanda Hall that has raised approximately C$120M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of four private peers in the same sector and stage: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M). This peer set reflects the broader frontier-energy investment landscape, which includes nine total private comparables and one public reference point (LNZA).

The peer-median method serves as the primary valuation anchor given data availability at this stage. The four same-sector, same-stage comparables yield a median valuation of $175M, with a range spanning $110M at the low end to $1.8B at the high end. This wide dispersion reflects the heterogeneity typical of early-stage climate tech—the $1.8B Watershed valuation appears as an outlier relative to the tighter clustering of the other three peers between $110M and $200M.

The triangulated valuation range is $149M to $201M, centered on the $175M median. This relatively narrow band applies where only one method is currently supported by sufficient data. Key uncertainties include whether Summit's technology differentiation and market positioning justify valuation toward the upper quartile ($200M+) or the median, and the degree to which the Watershed outlier reflects market exuberance versus genuine comparability gaps. Additional revenue traction or margin visibility would refine this range materially.
